children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: a .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy may not decommission or inactivate a battle force ship before the end of the expected service life of the ship.

the Secretary submits to the congressional defense committees the certification described in subsection (c) with respect to such ship by not later than three days after the date on which the President submits the budget materials under for the fiscal year in which such waiver is sought; andsection 1105(a) of title 31
a period of 30 days has elapsed following the date on which the National Defense Authorization Act for such fiscal year is enacted.

children.label: A
children.text: maintaining the battle force ship in a reduced operating status is not feasible;

children.label: B
children.text: maintaining the ship with reduced capability is not feasible;

children.label: C
children.text: maintaining the ship as a Navy Reserve unit is not feasible;

children.label: D
children.text: transferring the ship to the Coast Guard is not feasible; and

children.label: E
children.text: maintaining the ship is not required to support the most recent national defense strategy required by ; andsection 113(g) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the options assessed and the rationale for the determinations under subparagraphs (A) through (D) of paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the rationale for the determination under subparagraph (E) of such paragraph.

children.heading: .—Form
children.label: d .—Form
children.text: A certification submitted under subsection (b) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.

children.label: A
children.text: A commissioned United States Ship warship capable of contributing to combat operations.

children.label: B
children.text: A United States Naval Ship that contributes directly to Navy warfighting or support missions.
The term “expected service life” means the number of years a naval vessel is expected to be in service.
